---
title: "Willamette River Stream Temperature"
author: "Your Names Here"
date: today
format: html
---

## Overview

This document downloads (or loads) daily water temperature data from three USGS gauge
stations on the Willamette River in Oregon (2010–2023) and provides summary statistics
and a time-series visualization.

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(dataRetrieval)
library(tidyverse)
```

## Load or Download Data

Set `use_local <- TRUE` to load the saved CSV instead of hitting the NWIS server.

```{r}
#| label: load-data
#| message: false
#| warning: false

use_local <- TRUE   # change to FALSE to re-download from NWIS

if (use_local && file.exists("data/willamette_temp.csv")) {
  temp_data <- read_csv("data/willamette_temp.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
  temp_data$month <- factor(temp_data$month,
                            levels = c("Jan","Feb","Mar","Apr","May","Jun",
                                       "Jul","Aug","Sep","Oct","Nov","Dec"),
                            ordered = TRUE)
} else {
  siteNumbers <- c("14211720", "14211010", "14166000")
  parameterCd <- "00010"   # water temperature
  startDate   <- "2010-01-01"
  endDate     <- "2023-12-31"

  temp_data <- readNWISdv(siteNumbers, parameterCd, startDate, endDate)

  colnames(temp_data) <- c("agency_cd", "site_no", "Date", "temp_C")

  temp_data$month <- month(temp_data$Date, label = TRUE)
  temp_data$year  <- year(temp_data$Date)

  dir.create("data", showWarnings = FALSE)
  write_csv(temp_data, "data/willamette_temp.csv")
  message("Data downloaded and saved to data/willamette_temp.csv")
}
```

## Time-Series Plot

```{r}
#| label: plot-timeseries
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 4

ggplot(temp_data, aes(x = Date, y = temp_C, color = site_no)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(
    title = "Daily Stream Temperature at Willamette River Sites (2010–2023)",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Temperature (°C)",
    color = "Site"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Summary Statistics by Site

```{r}
#| label: summary-stats

temp_data |>
  group_by(site_no) |>
  summarise(
    n         = n(),
    mean_C    = round(mean(temp_C, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    min_C     = round(min(temp_C,  na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    max_C     = round(max(temp_C,  na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    sd_C      = round(sd(temp_C,   na.rm = TRUE), 2)
  ) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Summary statistics for each USGS site")
```
